entity_types.name: LiteraryWork
entity_types.description: A distinct creative work of literature, such as a book, play, or epic poem.

entity_types.name: Author
entity_types.description: The real-world person who wrote or created a literary work.

entity_types.name: Character
entity_types.description: A fictional person, creature, or entity that participates in the narrative of a literary work.

entity_types.name: Location
entity_types.description: A specific geographical place, building, region, or setting where events in a literary work occur.

entity_types.name: Object
entity_types.description: A significant, tangible item within a literary work's narrative, such as a weapon, vehicle, or treasure.

entity_types.name: Event
entity_types.description: A specific plot point, action, or occurrence that takes place within the timeline of a literary work.

entity_types.name: TextualComponent
entity_types.description: A structural division or part of a literary work's text, such as a chapter, act, scene, or volume.
examples: the 'To be, or not to be' soliloquy

entity_types.name: Theme
entity_types.description: An abstract concept, recurring idea, or central topic that is explored within a literary work.

reasoning: The taxonomy is designed to directly model the core entities required to answer the competency questions. A flat, non-hierarchical structure with eight distinct types was chosen to maximize clarity and simplify the entity extraction task. 'LiteraryWork' serves as the central entity. 'Author' and 'Character' are separated to distinguish real-world creators from fictional beings. 'Location', 'Object', and 'Event' capture the concrete 'where', 'what', and 'when' of the narrative plot, which is crucial for answering questions about setting, items, and chronology. 'TextualComponent' is included to handle queries about the physical structure of the texts (chapters, acts, scenes). Finally, 'Theme' supports abstract, analytical questions about the concepts and ideas within the works. This set of types provides comprehensive coverage for factual, structural, and thematic analysis of the literary corpus.
